feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Business Partner and Materiel Master querying too slow after remote client copy
entry.content:
I have performed a remote client copy between our PROD Sever (client 100) to our QUALITY Server (client 300) but since then we are experiencing slowness on some forms where you have to pickup Business Partner or Material Master. It can to take up to 5 to 10 minutes before the search wizard appears.

What should I have done after the client copy?

The weird part of all of this is that I am not able to see any error or related issue with this situation

entry.title: KUA funct. ‘Adapt UI’: add link poss.?
entry.content:
is it possible to use the KUA functionality ‘Adapt UI’ or a similar technique to create a constant text field on the user interface of any Fiori
in order to store the executable link to an online documentation there? Ideally, this would be in an entry on the right-hand side in the ‘Help Topics’ menu
under the ‘Learning’ entry - alternatively also on the Fiori interface in the top right-hand corner.



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Depreciation - Shift Wise Factor issue
entry.content:
Hi All,

Till now we were posting Depreciation - Shift Wise for Assets as unplanned but now we want to post the same system only. As per the requirement we are maintaining the Shift factor and Variable Depreciation portion in the required Asset Master - AS02 , but we are facing the following issues. We have ECC 6 and we are having V3 Fiscal Year Variant.

 

 

 

 

 

Requesting you all to please help me out as this is urgent.

With Regards,

Deepak Hamirani 

SAP S/4HANA Finance SAP Financial Information Management SAP Community

entry.title: CO08 - When I try to create a PO reference with SO using Tcode - CO08. CO object error
entry.content:
Problem Statement:

I am trying to create a production order with reference to a sales order using Tcode CO08. However, when executing the transaction, I encounter the following error:
"Sales order has no CO object."

I have attached a screenshot of the error message for reference.

Can anyone suggest how I can resolve this error and successfully create a production order?

entry.title: FIELD TO CLASIFY ORDER REQUEST (SOLPED)
entry.content:
Hi experts

The purchasing area requires that the purchase requisition includes a classification that makes it possible to identify whether it is a regularization, an emergency purchase or a normal purchase.

For this purpose, it is necessary to enable or use a standard field in SAP that meets the following characteristics:

Reportable in ME5A.
Predefined value list. This will avoid manual typing and reduce possible errors.
Mandatory field.

After exploring the available fields in the requisition, I identified that the “Requirement No.” field could be used for this purpose. However, this field does not meet all the necessary requirements. I have also considered the option of creating a new requisition type.

I would appreciate your guidance, based on your experience, to address this requirement in the most appropriate manner.

Thanks, Percy.

entry.title: How to Track Payment Term Changes in S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Does anyone have a way to track changes made in S/4 HANA to payment terms on all customers? A Report or a Fiori app that can track changes on all customers?

SAP S/4HANA FIN (Finance) SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting

entry.title: Multiple work item execution together not possible in SBWP
entry.content:
Hi,

 

We are trying to execute multiple work items in a single run. All the items are in the same group and the work item is dialog work items.

 

Regards.

entry.content:

How to add public view to  transport request and transport to QAS system?

entry.title: Configuring a Specific BOM Component for Exclusive Use in One Header Material within SAP S/4HANA BOM
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to know if there is a way to configure a specific component in SAP S/4HANA BOM (Bill of Materials) to be used exclusively in only one header material.

Could you provide detailed steps and settings required to achieve this?

Best regards,

entry.title: What are the SAP S/4HANA, On Premise and Private Edition, NCL SKUs for TDD?
entry.content:
Dear all,

What are the SAP S/4HANA, On Premise and Private Edition, Non-Commercial Licensing (NCL) SKUs for Test, Demo and Development (TDD) for SAP partners in Brazil?

Thanks,

FAFR

entry.title: Addressing Inventory Mismatch in Warehousing using SAP S/4HANA EWM: A Case Study from Germany
entry.content:
Inventory mismatch is one of the most persistent and impactful challenges faced by businesses across industries, ranging from e-commerce to manufacturing. It affects not only sales order fulfillment but also leads to significant financial losses when discrepancies in inventory valuation arise. From my past projects, I recall one such case involving a manufacturing giant in Germany ,which highlights the importance of effective warehouse management systems and ERP integration.

The Problem: Missing Stock During Transportation. The client, a leading manufacturer, operates multiple small warehouses alongside a central production plant. Despite using SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) to manage stock at the warehouse level, discrepancies frequently arose. Stock often went missing during the short transport journey between the production plant and the warehouse.

A site analysis revealed the root of the issue:

1. Absence of ERP at the Production Plant:

The production plant lacked an appropriate ERP system like SAP PP (Production Planning) to manage stock effectively. Instead, it relied on outdated, paper-based counting systems, which led to errors, delays, and a lack of transparency.

2. Manual Processes at the Warehouse Level:

Even at the warehouse level, manual practices such as paper-based inventory counting and bin sorting were prevalent. These processes not only contributed to inventory mismatches but also significantly hampered operational efficiency.

The Impact of Inventory Mismatch: The financial repercussions for the company were considerable. Missing stock meant not only a direct loss in inventory value but also disruptions in production and delayed order fulfillment. These inefficiencies cascaded across the supply chain, impacting customer satisfaction and the company’s bottom line.

The Solution: Implementing Robust SAP EWM Features.

To address these challenges, we implemented a series of measures leveraging SAP EWM’s capabilities:

1. SAP EWM Deconsolidation:

Deconsolidation is the process of breaking down large incoming shipments into smaller, more manageable units for efficient put-away and retrieval. By implementing SAP EWM's deconsolidation functionality, we ensured that products were properly sorted and organized before being stored in the warehouse. This streamlined the process and reduced errors during inventory handling.

2. SAP EWM Physical Inventory Counting:

We introduced SAP EWM’s physical inventory counting features to replace manual counting methods. This enabled automated and accurate inventory checks, reducing discrepancies and enhancing overall transparency.

3. SAP PP-EWM Integration at the Production Plant Level:

To address the lack of ERP at the production plant, we integrated SAP PP with SAP EWM. This integration provided real-time visibility into inventory movement from the production plant to the warehouse. It also automated key processes, ensuring seamless tracking and synchronization of stock levels.

Results Achieved:

The implementation of these solutions delivered several tangible benefits to the client:

Improved Transparency:

Real-time data flow between the production plant and the warehouse eliminated blind spots in inventory tracking.

Reduced Financial Losses:

By minimizing inventory mismatches, the company significantly reduced the financial impact of missing stock.

Enhanced Efficiency:

Automation replaced manual processes, speeding up operations and allowing employees to focus on higher-value tasks.

Better Order Fulfillment:

With accurate inventory data, the company could fulfill sales orders more reliably, improving customer satisfaction.

Key Takeaways:

This case underscores the critical importance of integrating robust ERP and warehouse management systems in addressing inventory mismatches. Here are some key lessons learned:

1. ERP Integration is Essential:

A lack of ERP tools at the production plant was the primary cause of the issue. Integrating SAP PP with SAP EWM provided the necessary transparency and control.

2. Eliminate Manual Processes:

Paper-based systems are prone to errors and inefficiencies. Transitioning to automated processes not only reduces discrepancies but also enhances operational efficiency.

3. Leverage Advanced Features of SAP EWM:

Functions like deconsolidation and physical inventory counting are powerful tools for optimizing warehouse operations. Companies should utilize these features to address specific challenges.

4. Adopt a Holistic Approach:

Warehouse management doesn’t exist in isolation. Issues at the production plant level can ripple through the supply chain. A holistic approach, addressing both production and warehousing, is crucial.

Conclusion: 

Inventory mismatch is a common yet solvable issue that affects businesses across industries. By adopting the right technologies and integrating processes, companies can mitigate losses, improve efficiency, and enhance customer satisfaction. The German manufacturing giant’s case serves as a testament to the transformative impact of leveraging SAP EWM and ERP tools effectively. For organizations looking to streamline their operations, this approach provides a blueprint for success.#SAPEWM SAP Supply Chain Management  SAP Extended Warehouse Management SAP ERP, add-on for embedded production planning and detailed scheduling

entry.title: When install SAP Solution Manager 7.2 SR 2,DVEBMGS02 [ABAP: ACTIVE] did not start
entry.content:
An error occurred while processing option SAP Solution Manager 7.2 Support Release 2

 

So I can not install SAP Solution Manager 7.2 Support Release 2  

 

ctsrvs4mc01:/ # su - smaadm
ctsrvs4mc01:smaadm 301> sapcontrol -nr 02 -function GetProcessList

30.12.2024 07:53:54
GetProcessList
OK
name, description, dispstatus, textstatus, starttime, elapsedtime, pid
disp+work, Dispatcher, GREEN, Running, 2024 12 29 22:41:36, 9:12:18, 11015
igswd_mt, IGS Watchdog, GRAY, Stopped, , , 11016
gwrd, Gateway, GREEN, Running, 2024 12 29 22:41:37, 9:12:17, 11018
icman, ICM, GREEN, Running, 2024 12 29 22:41:37, 9:12:17, 11019
ctsrvs4mc01:smaadm 302>

entry.title: SAP Setup for Single Product with Multiple Machine Configurations
entry.content: "We have a single product that is produced on two separate machines, Machine A and Machine B, which have different configurations. Machine A is equipped with 4 filling nozzles, while Machine B is equipped with 16 filling nozzles. The production process and resource utilization, including labor and machine hours, vary depending on the machine used. I would like to know how to configure SAP to accommodate this variability in production and accurately capture the differences in labor and machine hours consumed on each machine."

entry.title: In Migo can someone kindly advise which movement code I need to for transit stock to unrestricted?
entry.content: Can someone kindly advise me on which movement code I need for moving materials from transit stock to unrestricted stock in Migo?

entry.title: HANA update to revision 81
entry.content:
Current environment:
OS :suse15sp2
DB :PERSON_NAME IP_ADDRESS
Target environment for upgrade:
OS :suse15sp6
DB :PERSON_NAME 2.0 revision 81
We want to upgrade the current operating system to suse15sp6, according to DIGITS- SAP PERSON_NAME: Supported Operating Systems， Should I upgrade the database to revision 59.12 first, and then upgrade the database to revision 80 after the OS is upgraded to suse15sp6.

Question and Answer:
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: PERSON_NAME Software

Q: SAP PERSON_NAME Revision number and Operating System where software is being installed
A:

Q: Software being upgraded (source and target)?
A:

Q: When did the issue last occur (MMM,DD, YYYY)
A:


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAP MDG: For Change CR Type - while changing any field data Error Message to be perform
entry.content:
Hi

For Change CR Type - soon after clicking on edit on Change Request & if any of the field getting change, error message should get display.

How to perform this for above, Like how to get the changed fields data before submit of CR ? 
From my side tried several methods to get, but nothing worked.

Thanks & Regards, 
Sandeep Suggu.
(SAP MDG Consultant)

SAP Master Data Governance SAP S/4HANA

entry.title: CDS Views during upgrade
entry.content: Is it possible to overlook CDS view activation errors during the ACT_UPG phase of SUM? CDS views are built on top of the DDIC so should be treated as Development objects and not DDIC objects. We have encountered various errors related to Cds views in this SUM phase and think that the shadow client is not the correct place to fix these items. If you have any experience with SAP upgrades and have encountered such issues please let me know your views.

entry.title: Split batch, Picking and PGI using single IDOC (SHPCON) for Outbound delivery
entry.content: I want to batch split items, pick, pack, and perform PGI in a single IDOC (SHPCON). At present, I am unable to do so in a single IDOC. However, I can perform a batch split in one IDOC using the segment E1EDL18-QUAF = 'CHG', and in another IDOC, I can handle picking, packing, and PGI using the segments E1EDL18-QUAF = 'PIC' and E1EDL18-QUAF = 'PGI', but not all in a single IDOC. Need some urgent help.

entry.title: How to implement additional booking periods (More than 12) in SAC and Datasphere?
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are currently working on a reporting using SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere with data coming from S/4HANA. 

In S/4HANA it is possible to work with additional booking periods (More than the typical 12 month booking periods). We have the requirement to report these additional booking periods using SAP Analytics Cloud and Datasphere. As there is barely any information or blogs regarding this and no standard solution available in SAC/Datasphere, we were wondering if someone worked on a similar solution before or if there are any best practices for this kind of implementation.

Any help is much appreciated!

Best regards

entry.title: PY-CA-YE 2024 Tax forms should NOT contain /317 in QPP Base amount boxes B02 and B17
entry.content:
PY-CA-YE 2024 Tax forms should not contain /317 in Base amount boxes

Wage type apps R124 and T424 are affected. Hydro Quebec has removed /317 from the noted boxes. This message is to advise SAP of this need and ensure further Notes do not add this wage type back in.

The RL1 guide states only the base amount and the first additional QPP are to be reported in B02. This first additional amount was defined and described in Notes 3248911 and 3283966. We have validated this with our Tax specialist and provide the attachment for detailed information. Hydro Quebec has removed /317 from the affected boxes to continue testing our year end processing of tax forms.
